articles.headline: India Launches Phase II of National Logistics Corridor Project, Boosting Economic Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today inaugurated Phase II of the ambitious National Logistics Corridor Project, focusing on enhancing multimodal connectivity across key industrial and agricultural hubs.
• This phase targets critical infrastructure development, including dedicated freight corridors, port connectivity enhancements, and integrated logistics parks.
• The project aims to significantly reduce logistics costs, improve supply chain efficiency, and boost India's competitiveness in global trade.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging technology for real-time tracking, data analytics, and seamless coordination among various transport modes.
• The initiative is expected to generate substantial employment opportunities and catalyze regional economic development.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Corridor Project aims to reduce logistics costs to global benchmarks.
Phase II focuses on specific geographical corridors and modal integration.
Key components include roads, railways, ports, and digital platforms.
Ministry of Commerce and Industry is the nodal ministry for logistics policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic benefits of improved logistics infrastructure for India's GDP growth and export competitiveness.
Analyze the role of multimodal transport in achieving sustainable and efficient supply chains.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale infrastructure projects like the National Logistics Corridor and suggest mitigation strategies.
Evaluate the project's potential impact on regional disparities and employment generation.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized map showing new logistics corridors connecting major industrial cities and ports in India.
articles.trivia: India's logistics cost as a percentage of GDP is significantly higher than global averages, making such projects crucial for economic efficiency.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' in Landmark Digital Privacy Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a significant judgment, affirming the 'Right to be Forgotten' in specific contexts of digital privacy and personal data protection.
• The ruling mandates search engines and online platforms to delist or de-index certain personal information upon request, particularly when it is outdated, irrelevant, or causes undue harm.
• The Court balanced the individual's right to privacy against freedom of speech and expression, emphasizing the need for a case-by-case assessment.
• This decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for data protection laws and the obligations of online intermediaries in India.
• It reinforces the principles laid down in the Puttaswamy judgment regarding the right to privacy as a fundamental right.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: Right to be Forgotten is a concept originating from European Union's GDPR.
Puttaswamy judgment declared right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21.
The ruling applies to outdated, irrelevant, or harmful personal information.
Balance struck between privacy and freedom of expression.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Supreme Court's ruling on the 'Right to be Forgotten' in strengthening India's digital privacy framework.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the 'Right to be Forgotten' for online platforms and search engines.
Compare India's approach to data privacy with international standards like GDPR.
Examine the implications of this judgment on freedom of speech and expression in the digital age.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents with a digital screen displaying privacy icons in the background.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' gained prominence after a 2014 European Court of Justice ruling against Google.

articles.headline: RBI's Financial Stability Report Highlights Resilience Amidst Global Headwinds, Calls for Vigilance
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today released its Financial Stability Report (FSR) for Q3 2024, assessing the health of India's financial system.
• The report noted continued resilience in the banking sector, characterized by improving asset quality and robust capital adequacy ratios.
• It cautioned against lingering global headwinds, including geopolitical tensions, commodity price volatility, and potential spillovers from advanced economies' monetary policies.
• The FSR emphasized the need for continued vigilance by financial institutions and regulators to address emerging risks, particularly in the non-banking financial company (NBFC) sector and digital lending space.
• Macroeconomic stability and structural reforms were identified as key factors contributing to India's financial robustness.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Financial Stability Report (FSR) is published bi-annually by RBI.
It assesses risks to the financial system.
Key indicators include Gross Non-Performing Assets (GNPA) and Capital to Risk-weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR).
Global headwinds mentioned include geopolitical tensions and commodity prices.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key takeaways from the latest RBI Financial Stability Report regarding the health of India's banking and financial sectors.
Discuss the challenges posed by global economic uncertainties and how India is positioned to mitigate these risks.
Evaluate the role of regulatory vigilance, particularly in the NBFC and digital lending segments, for maintaining financial stability.
Suggest policy measures to further strengthen India's financial system against future shocks.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing economic indicators with an RBI logo in the background.
articles.trivia: The first Financial Stability Report was published by the RBI in March 2010.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership Review Emphasizes Maritime Security and Trade Facilitation
articles.explainer:
• A high-level review of the India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership concluded today, reaffirming commitment to deeper cooperation across various sectors.
• Key discussions focused on enhancing maritime security cooperation, including joint exercises and information sharing, to ensure a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Both sides stressed the importance of boosting trade and investment, with a renewed push for simplifying trade procedures and exploring new avenues for economic engagement.
• Cultural exchanges, tourism promotion, and cooperation in digital transformation were also identified as priority areas for future collaboration.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy, underscoring its commitment to strengthen ties with the ASEAN region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization of ten Southeast Asian countries.
India became a Sectoral Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a Strategic Partner in 2012.
India's 'Act East Policy' is a successor to the 'Look East Policy'.
Maritime security and trade are key pillars of India-ASEAN cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership in the context of India's 'Act East Policy' and regional geopolitics.
Discuss the potential for enhanced cooperation between India and ASEAN in maritime security and economic integration.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in deepening its engagement with the ASEAN bloc.
Evaluate how India's relationship with ASEAN contributes to its vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states arranged together.
articles.trivia: ASEAN's motto is 'One Vision, One Identity, One Community'.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious Green Hydrogen Mission Targets for 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy today announced enhanced targets under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for significant production capacity by 2030.
• The revised targets include a substantial increase in green hydrogen production, reduction in GHG emissions, and creation of export opportunities for green hydrogen and its derivatives.
• The mission emphasizes incentives for manufacturing electrolyzers, developing green hydrogen production capabilities, and establishing green hydrogen hubs.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to achieve net-zero emissions by 2070 and establish itself as a global leader in green energy.
• Investments in research and development, skill development, and pilot projects for green hydrogen applications are also integral to the mission.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Infrastructure: Energy.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Targets include reducing fossil fuel imports and creating export opportunities.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of green hydrogen as a key driver for India's energy transition and achieving its net-zero targets.
Discuss the economic and environmental benefits of scaling up green hydrogen production in India.
Examine the challenges in developing a robust green hydrogen ecosystem, including infrastructure, technology, and cost, and suggest policy interventions.
Evaluate India's position as a potential global leader in green hydrogen production and export.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing a plant producing green hydrogen with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but green hydrogen production is energy-intensive.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype, Eyes Cost-Effective Space Access
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a critical test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a precise landing demonstration, validating key technologies for autonomous navigation, guidance, and control necessary for future orbital re-entry and landing missions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing fully reusable launch systems, which are crucial for reducing space launch costs and enhancing India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• ISRO aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) RLV capable of ferrying satellites and potentially humans to space.
• The indigenous development of RLV technology underscores India's growing capabilities in advanced space engineering.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is ISRO's primary project for reusable launch vehicles.
The test focuses on hypersonic flight and autonomous landing capabilities.
Reusable launch vehicles aim to reduce the cost of space access.
ISRO is developing various technologies for future human spaceflight missions (Gaganyaan).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program.
Analyze how RLVs can revolutionize space access by reducing costs and increasing mission flexibility.
Examine the challenges and technological hurdles ISRO faces in developing a fully operational RLV.
Evaluate the potential impact of indigenous RLV technology on India's position in the global space market.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle descending for a landing.
articles.trivia: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is currently one of the most successful operational reusable orbital rockets.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Approves Procurement of Advanced Indigenous Electronic Warfare Systems
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, today approved proposals for the procurement of advanced indigenous Electronic Warfare (EW) systems.
• These systems are designed to enhance the Indian Armed Forces' capabilities in intelligence gathering, communication jamming, and protecting friendly forces from enemy electronic attacks.
• The focus on indigenous development aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in defence, reducing reliance on foreign suppliers and boosting domestic defence manufacturing.
• The procurement is aimed at modernizing the armed forces and ensuring superiority in the evolving spectrum of warfare.
• This decision is expected to give a significant thrust to private sector participation in defence production and R&D.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life (Defence Technology).
prelimsPoints: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body for defence procurement.
Electronic Warfare involves the use of electromagnetic spectrum to control the battlefield.
Indigenous procurement is a key pillar of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defence.
EW systems are crucial for intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR).
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of Electronic Warfare (EW) systems in modern conventional and unconventional warfare scenarios.
Analyze how the indigenization of defence procurement, particularly in high-tech areas like EW, contributes to India's strategic autonomy.
Examine the challenges faced by the domestic defence manufacturing sector in meeting the advanced technological requirements of the armed forces.
Evaluate the role of private sector participation in strengthening India's defence industrial base.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of electronic signals and radar waves, symbolizing electronic warfare.
articles.trivia: The first use of electronic warfare can be traced back to the Russo-Japanese War of 1904-05.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Launches New Digital Health Platform for Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) today unveiled a new comprehensive digital health platform specifically designed to enhance healthcare access and delivery in rural and remote areas.
• The platform integrates teleconsultation services, electronic health records (EHRs), and medication delivery networks, aiming to bridge the urban-rural healthcare divide.
• It will facilitate remote diagnostics, specialist consultations, and enable frontline health workers to efficiently manage patient data and outreach programs.
• The initiative is a significant step towards achieving Universal Health Coverage and leveraging technology for equitable healthcare access across India.
• Training programs for healthcare professionals and community health workers on using the new digital tools are also being rolled out.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by subsuming NRHM and NUHM.
The new platform focuses on rural and remote areas.
Key features include teleconsultation and EHRs.
Aims to bridge the urban-rural healthcare gap.
mainsPoints: Discuss how digital health platforms can transform healthcare delivery in rural India, addressing issues of accessibility and quality.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of electronic health records (EHRs) in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate the role of technology in achieving Universal Health Coverage and strengthening primary healthcare systems.
Suggest measures to ensure digital literacy and infrastructure availability for the effective implementation of such initiatives in remote areas.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor conducting a teleconsultation with a rural patient on a digital tablet.
articles.trivia: India's eSanjeevani platform has already facilitated millions of teleconsultations, particularly during the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.headline: Government Revises Minimum Support Price (MSP) for Rabi Crops, Focus on Farmer Income and Diversification
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved a revision in the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for all mandated Rabi crops for the upcoming marketing season.
• The hike aims to ensure remunerative prices for farmers, encouraging crop diversification and promoting sustainable agricultural practices.
• The revised MSP is set at least 1.5 times the All-India weighted average cost of production, as per the government's commitment.
• This decision is expected to boost farmer incomes, incentivize higher production of specific crops, and contribute to food security.
• Special emphasis has been placed on pulses and oilseeds to reduce India's import dependency and promote self-sufficiency.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security.
Economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: MSP is announced by the Government of India for 22 mandated crops and Sugarcane (FRP).
Rabi crops are sown in winter and harvested in spring (e.g., wheat, barley, gram).
Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends MSPs.
The MSP is set at least 1.5 times the All-India weighted average cost of production (A2+FL).
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and implications of the government's revision of MSP for Rabi crops, particularly concerning farmer welfare and agricultural sustainability.
Analyze the role of MSP in incentivizing crop diversification and reducing India's import dependency on pulses and oilseeds.
Examine the challenges associated with the MSP regime, such as market distortions and fiscal burden, and suggest reforms.
Evaluate the effectiveness of MSP as a tool for ensuring remunerative prices to farmers and achieving food security.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field of wheat, with a symbolic representation of MSP calculations.
articles.trivia: The concept of MSP was introduced in India in the mid-1960s to provide price support to farmers and encourage higher production.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Comprehensive UNSC Reforms at Global Forum, Cites Changing Geopolitical Realities
articles.explainer:
• India today strongly reiterated its call for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) at a multilateral diplomatic forum.
• The Indian representative highlighted that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect contemporary geopolitical realities and the increased role of developing nations.
• India advocated for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent seats, emphasizing the need for greater representation from Africa, Latin America, and Asia.
• The call for reforms aims to make the UNSC more legitimate, effective, and representative of the 21st-century global order.
• India stressed its commitment to multilateralism and the urgent need for a more equitable global governance structure.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
India is a strong contender for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocate for permanent seats.
Veto power is held by the P5 members.
mainsPoints: Analyze the compelling reasons for India's persistent demand for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
Discuss the challenges and obstacles in achieving UNSC reforms, considering the interests of existing permanent members.
Evaluate the potential impact of an expanded and reformed UNSC on global peace and security, particularly for developing nations.
Examine India's role and contributions to global governance that strengthen its case for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations building in New York with flags, symbolizing international diplomacy.
articles.trivia: The last major reform of the UN Security Council took place in 1965, when the number of non-permanent members was increased from 6 to 10.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Strengthening Data Protection Framework, Calls for Independent Regulator
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) today submitted its report on the proposed Data Protection Bill, recommending several key amendments to strengthen India's data protection framework.
• The committee emphasized the need for a robust and independent data protection authority with significant enforcement powers.
• Recommendations include tighter regulations on cross-border data flows, stricter consent requirements for data processing, and enhanced penalties for data breaches.
• The report also addressed concerns regarding government access to personal data, suggesting clearer guidelines and oversight mechanisms.
• This report is crucial for the finalization of India's long-awaited Personal Data Protection Bill.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Data Protection Bill aims to protect the privacy of individuals' data.
JPC scrutinizes bills and provides recommendations.
An independent Data Protection Authority (DPA) is a key recommendation.
Cross-border data flows and consent mechanisms are critical aspects.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee on the Data Protection Bill and their significance for safeguarding digital privacy in India.
Discuss the role and importance of an independent data protection authority in regulating data processing and ensuring accountability.
Examine the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights in the context of data access by the government.
Evaluate how a robust data protection framework can foster trust in India's digital economy and promote innovation.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred image of parliamentary proceedings with a focus on a document titled 'Data Protection Report'.
articles.trivia: The first draft of India's Data Protection Bill was based on the recommendations of the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee.

articles.headline: Jal Shakti Ministry Initiates New Project for Urban Water Recycling and Reuse
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti today launched a new nationwide project aimed at promoting urban wastewater recycling and reuse for non-potable purposes.
• The initiative targets reducing the freshwater demand in cities by treating and utilizing municipal wastewater for industrial processes, irrigation, and groundwater recharge.
• It involves upgrading existing Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) with advanced technologies and constructing new ones with a focus on resource recovery.
• The project aligns with the principles of circular economy and sustainable water management, addressing growing urban water scarcity.
• Public awareness campaigns and financial incentives for adopting recycled water by industries and local bodies are also part of the plan.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Water Resources.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Jal Shakti was formed by merging Ministry of Water Resources, River Development & Ganga Rejuvenation and Ministry of Drinking Water & Sanitation.
The project focuses on non-potable reuse of treated wastewater.
STPs are crucial for treating municipal wastewater.
Aims to reduce freshwater demand and promote groundwater recharge.
mainsPoints: Discuss the urgency and importance of urban wastewater recycling and reuse in addressing India's escalating water crisis.
Analyze the technical, financial, and social challenges in implementing large-scale urban water recycling projects in India.
Evaluate how the principles of a circular economy can be effectively applied to urban water management for sustainable development.
Suggest policy measures and technological innovations required to boost the adoption of treated wastewater for various purposes.
articles.imageDescription: A diagram illustrating the process of wastewater treatment and its reuse for various applications.
articles.trivia: Globally, only about 20% of wastewater is treated before being discharged, leading to significant environmental pollution.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Develop Novel Diagnostic Tool for Neglected Tropical Diseases
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists from the National Institute of Biomedical Research has announced the development of a novel, rapid, and cost-effective diagnostic tool for several neglected tropical diseases (NTDs).
• The new diagnostic kit uses advanced biosensor technology to detect specific biomarkers, offering quicker and more accurate results compared to existing methods.
• This breakthrough is crucial for early detection, effective treatment, and control of NTDs, which disproportionately affect vulnerable populations in tropical and subtropical regions.
• The indigenous development of this technology aligns with India's commitment to public health and scientific innovation.
• Clinical trials have shown high sensitivity and specificity, paving the way for its widespread deployment in affected areas.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: NTDs are a diverse group of communicable diseases prevalent in tropical and subtropical regions.
The new tool uses biosensor technology for rapid detection.
Early detection is crucial for NTD control.
India aims for elimination of several NTDs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic burden of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and the importance of rapid diagnostic tools for their control.
Analyze how indigenous scientific innovations, like the new diagnostic tool, contribute to strengthening public health infrastructure.
Examine the challenges in deploying advanced medical technologies in remote and underserved areas affected by NTDs.
Suggest policy measures to accelerate research and development in NTDs and ensure equitable access to diagnostic and treatment options.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat holding a diagnostic kit, with a microscope in the background.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) lists 20 diseases as Neglected Tropical Diseases.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Nari Shakti Udyam' Scheme to Promote Women Entrepreneurship and Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development today launched 'Nari Shakti Udyam', a new scheme aimed at fostering women entrepreneurship and economic empowerment across India.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill development training, and mentorship support to women aspiring to start or expand their businesses.
• It focuses on facilitating access to credit, market linkages, and digital tools for women entrepreneurs, particularly in rural and semi-urban areas.
• 'Nari Shakti Udyam' seeks to break down socio-economic barriers and create a supportive ecosystem for women to achieve financial independence.
• Partnerships with banks, self-help groups (SHGs), and industry associations are integral to the scheme's implementation.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Nodal Ministry is the Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Scheme offers financial aid, skill training, and mentorship.
Aims to promote women's economic independence.
Focus on rural and semi-urban women entrepreneurs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of promoting women entrepreneurship for India's socio-economic development and achieving gender equality.
Analyze how schemes like 'Nari Shakti Udyam' address the unique challenges faced by women entrepreneurs in accessing finance, markets, and skills.
Evaluate the role of self-help groups (SHGs) and digital platforms in empowering women economically.
Suggest further policy interventions and societal changes required to create a truly enabling environment for women entrepreneurs in India.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse women entrepreneurs confidently discussing business, symbolizing empowerment.
articles.trivia: India's MUDRA Yojana has been instrumental in providing collateral-free loans to micro-enterprises, with a significant portion going to women beneficiaries.

articles.headline: Digital Transactions in India Reach New Peak, Driven by Robust UPI Adoption
articles.explainer:
• India's digital payments ecosystem witnessed unprecedented growth, with transactions reaching a new peak in the last quarter, primarily fueled by the widespread adoption of the Unified Payments Interface (UPI).
• The surge in digital transactions reflects increasing financial inclusion, growing consumer confidence in digital platforms, and supportive government policies.
• UPI continues to be the dominant payment method, facilitating billions of transactions monthly, encompassing peer-to-peer and merchant payments.
• This digital revolution is transforming India into a less-cash economy, enhancing transparency and efficiency in financial flows.
• The growth also highlights the success of the 'Digital India' initiative and the robust public digital infrastructure built over the years.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life (Digital Technology).
prelimsPoints: UPI is developed by National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
It allows instant real-time payments between bank accounts.
India leads globally in real-time digital payments.
Growth indicates increasing financial inclusion and digital literacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of UPI and other digital payment systems on India's economy and financial inclusion landscape.
Analyze the factors contributing to the unprecedented growth of digital transactions in India and its implications for the 'less-cash' economy.
Examine the challenges associated with digital payments, such as cybersecurity risks and digital divide, and suggest mitigation strategies.
Evaluate the role of public digital infrastructure in driving innovation and entrepreneurship in the fintech sector.
articles.imageDescription: A hand holding a smartphone displaying a UPI payment successful message, with Indian currency notes blurred in the background.
articles.trivia: UPI was launched in 2016 and has since become one of the world's most successful real-time payment systems.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Site in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) today announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist site during excavations in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The site includes remnants of stupas, viharas, and intricate sculptures, providing new insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India.
• Early findings suggest the site dates back to the Gupta and post-Gupta periods, indicating a thriving Buddhist monastic complex in the region.
• This discovery contributes valuable evidence to understanding the rich cultural and religious heritage of Odisha and its connections with ancient Buddhist centers.
• Further excavations are expected to reveal more about the architectural styles, daily life, and religious practices of the period.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Viharas are Buddhist monasteries.
Odisha has several prominent Buddhist sites like Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri (Diamond Triangle).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding the spread and evolution of Buddhism in India, with special reference to Eastern India.
Analyze how such findings contribute to rewriting or refining existing historical narratives about ancient Indian kingdoms and their cultural interactions.
Examine the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and documenting ancient sites amidst rapid urbanization and development.
Evaluate the role of archaeology in promoting cultural tourism and heritage awareness in India.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist stupa with intricate carvings, under the supervision of archaeologists.
articles.trivia: The famous Buddhist sites of Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri in Odisha collectively form the 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha's Buddhist heritage.

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues High-Alert for New Ransomware Variant Targeting Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) today issued a high-severity alert regarding a sophisticated new ransomware variant targeting critical information infrastructure (CII) and government networks.
• The ransomware, dubbed 'CipherLock', employs advanced encryption techniques and evasion tactics, posing a significant threat to data integrity and operational continuity.
• CERT-In has advised all organizations, particularly those in critical sectors like energy, finance, and telecommunications, to immediately implement enhanced cybersecurity measures.
• Recommendations include regular data backups, network segmentation, multi-factor authentication, and employee training on phishing awareness.
• The alert underscores the escalating threat landscape in cyberspace and the need for robust national cyber resilience strategies.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Ransomware encrypts data and demands payment for decryption.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to vital assets whose disruption could have severe impact.
Cyber resilience is the ability to withstand and recover from cyberattacks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of ransomware attacks on critical infrastructure and government networks, and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the challenges faced by India in building a robust cybersecurity framework and ensuring cyber resilience across various sectors.
Evaluate the effectiveness of measures like network segmentation, multi-factor authentication, and employee training in mitigating ransomware risks.
Suggest policy and institutional reforms required to enhance India's capabilities in preventing, detecting, and responding to sophisticated cyber threats.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon overlaid on a network diagram, symbolizing cybersecurity threats.
articles.trivia: The WannaCry ransomware attack in 2017 affected over 200,000 computers in 150 countries, including India.

articles.headline: Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) Phase 2 Focuses on Circular Economy for Waste Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today announced a renewed focus on circular economy principles under Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) Phase 2.
• The initiative aims to transform waste into valuable resources through enhanced segregation at source, advanced processing technologies, and promoting reuse and recycling.
• Key objectives include achieving 100% waste processing, remediation of legacy dumpsites, and making cities 'garbage-free' through a holistic approach.
• Emphasis is placed on citizen participation, behavioral change, and strengthening the capacity of urban local bodies in sustainable waste management.
• The move is expected to significantly reduce environmental pollution, improve public health, and create green jobs in the waste management sector.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) Phase 2 was launched in 2021.
Focus on 'Garbage Free Cities' and wastewater management.
Circular economy principles involve reducing, reusing, and recycling.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the shift towards a circular economy approach in Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) Phase 2 can address India's growing urban waste crisis effectively.
Analyze the challenges in implementing comprehensive waste segregation, processing, and recycling mechanisms across diverse Indian cities.
Evaluate the role of citizen participation, behavioral change, and technological adoption in achieving 'garbage-free' cities.
Suggest policy measures to incentivize private sector involvement and innovation in sustainable waste management practices.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing different types of waste being segregated into bins for recycling and composting, with city skyline in background.
articles.trivia: The Swachh Bharat Mission was launched on October 2, 2014, to achieve universal sanitation coverage.

articles.headline: States Adopt Unified E-Governance Portal for Seamless Public Services Delivery
articles.explainer:
• Several Indian states today announced the successful integration and adoption of a unified e-governance portal to provide a wide range of public services to citizens.
• The portal aims to eliminate bureaucratic hurdles, enhance transparency, and ensure efficient, time-bound delivery of government services online.
• Services include applications for various certificates, utility bill payments, land records access, and grievance redressal mechanisms.
• This initiative is part of the broader 'Digital India' vision to transform governance and improve ease of living for citizens.
• Interoperability between different state departments and central government services is a key feature, promoting a 'whole-of-government' approach.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: E-governance aims to make government services accessible, efficient, and transparent.
Digital India is a flagship program to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
The unified portal facilitates citizen-centric service delivery.
Interoperability is key for seamless service provision.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of unified e-governance portals in enhancing public services delivery, transparency, and accountability in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by states in implementing and integrating diverse government services onto a single digital platform.
Evaluate how e-governance initiatives contribute to improving the 'ease of living' for citizens and promoting good governance.
Suggest measures to bridge the digital divide and ensure equitable access to e-governance services across all sections of society.
articles.imageDescription: A laptop screen displaying a government e-service portal, with hands typing on the keyboard.
articles.trivia: The National e-Governance Plan (NeGP) was launched in 2006 with the vision of 'Make all Government services accessible to the common man'.

articles.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Antarctic Research and Climate Change
articles.explainer:
• India today hosted a major international conference focusing on Antarctic research, climate change impacts, and sustainable governance of the polar region.
• Scientists and policymakers from around the globe participated, sharing insights on glaciology, oceanography, biodiversity, and the role of Antarctica in global climate systems.
• The conference emphasized the critical importance of international collaboration in polar science and adherence to the Antarctic Treaty System.
• Discussions also covered the implications of polar ice melt for global sea levels and its impact on coastal nations, including India.
• India reiterated its commitment to peaceful scientific research in Antarctica and protecting its pristine environment.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life (Polar Science).
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India is a signatory to the Antarctic Treaty System.
India has two operational research stations in Antarctica: Maitri and Bharati.
Dakshin Gangotri was India's first permanent research station, now decommissioned.
Antarctica plays a crucial role in regulating global climate.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's scientific research in Antarctica and its contribution to global understanding of climate change.
Analyze the environmental and geopolitical challenges associated with the sustainable governance and resource management of the Antarctic region.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and the Antarctic Treaty System in maintaining peace and promoting scientific research in the continent.
Examine the implications of polar ice melt for India's coastal regions and its food security.
articles.imageDescription: A research station amidst snow-covered mountains in Antarctica, with an Indian flag flying.
articles.trivia: India launched its first expedition to Antarctica in 1981, establishing Dakshin Gangotri in 1983.

highlights: India launches Phase II of National Logistics Corridor Project to boost economic connectivity and reduce logistics costs.
Supreme Court upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' in digital privacy cases, setting a precedent for data protection in India.
RBI's Financial Stability Report for Q3 2024 highlights banking sector resilience but cautions against global headwinds.
Government unveils ambitious Green Hydrogen Mission targets for 2030, aiming for significant production capacity and net-zero goals.
CERT-In issues high-alert for a new ransomware variant targeting critical infrastructure, urging enhanced cybersecurity measures.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for the production and export of Green Hydrogen.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
3. The Ministry of Power is the nodal ministry for the implementation of this mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is correct: Green hydrogen is indeed produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources, making it a clean fuel. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry for the implementation of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, not the Ministry of Power.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for recommending the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for various crops in India?
options: (b) Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
(c) Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) is an attached office of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare. It is mandated to recommend MSPs for 22 mandated crops and fair and remunerative price (FRP) for sugarcane. The final decision on MSP is taken by the Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA).

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India:
1. It is explicitly enshrined as a fundamental right under Article 19 of the Indian Constitution.
2. The Supreme Court of India has affirmed its applicability, balancing it with freedom of speech and expression.
3. It primarily allows individuals to demand delisting of outdated or irrelevant personal information from online platforms.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The 'Right to be Forgotten' is not explicitly enshrined under Article 19. While the Supreme Court has recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 (Puttaswamy judgment), the 'Right to be Forgotten' is a derivative right that finds its basis in Article 21. Statement 2 is correct: The Supreme Court has indeed affirmed its applicability, noting the need to balance it with other fundamental rights like freedom of speech and expression. Statement 3 is correct: The essence of the 'Right to be Forgotten' is to allow individuals to request removal or delisting of personal data that is no longer relevant, accurate, or excessive.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a permanent research station of India in Antarctica?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Maitri and Bharati are India's two active permanent research stations in Antarctica. Dakshin Gangotri was India's first permanent station, established in 1983, but it was decommissioned as a fully functional research station in 1990 and converted into a supply base. Sagar Nidhi is an Indian ice-class research vessel, not a permanent research station in Antarctica.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Unified Payments Interface' (UPI) is often seen in news in the context of:
options: (b) A digital payment system in India
(d) A framework for cyber security protocols
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) A digital payment system in India
prelimsMocks.explanation: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is an instant real-time payment system developed by National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) for inter-bank transactions. It is a widely used digital payment system in India.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the significance of the National Logistics Corridor Project for India's economic growth and global competitiveness. Discuss the challenges in its implementation and suggest measures to overcome them. (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.; Investment models; Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Logistics Corridor Project and its objectives. Discuss its significance for economic growth (reduced costs, increased trade, efficiency, employment) and global competitiveness (Make in India, export boost). Detail implementation challenges (land acquisition, funding, inter-agency coordination, technology adoption, last-mile connectivity). Suggest measures like single-window clearance, innovative financing, PPP models, skill development, and digital integration.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's affirmation of the 'Right to be Forgotten' marks a critical juncture in India's journey towards a robust digital privacy regime. Discuss the implications of this ruling for individual privacy, online intermediaries, and the future of data protection legislation in India. (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections; Fundamental Rights.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by explaining the 'Right to be Forgotten' and its basis in the right to privacy (Article 21). Discuss implications for individuals (control over personal data, reputation management) and online intermediaries (new obligations, technical challenges, balancing with freedom of expression). Analyze its impact on the Personal Data Protection Bill and the need for a strong, independent regulator. Conclude on the evolving landscape of digital rights in India.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to position the country as a global leader in green energy. Critically evaluate the potential of green hydrogen as a sustainable energy source and the challenges India faces in scaling up its production and adoption. (15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Infrastructure: Energy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Discuss the potential of green hydrogen as a sustainable energy source (zero emissions, energy security, export potential, diverse applications). Critically evaluate challenges (high production cost, infrastructure development for storage/transport, technological advancements, water availability, energy demand for electrolysis). Suggest strategies like R&D investment, policy incentives, international collaborations, and demand creation.

quickBytes.text: The 'Sagarmala Programme' aims to promote port-led development in India.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
The 'Parijaat' tree, also known as Night-flowering Jasmine, is the official flower of Uttar Pradesh.
India is the world's third-largest producer of renewable energy.
The 'Gaganyaan' mission is India's first human spaceflight program.
The 'Golden Quadrilateral' is a network of highways connecting India's four major metropolitan cities.
The 'Global Hunger Index' is published annually by Concern Worldwide and Welthungerhilfe.
The 'National Skill Development Corporation' (NSDC) is a not-for-profit public limited company.
The 'Antarctic Treaty' reserves the continent for peaceful, scientific purposes.
The 'Chola Temples' of Thanjavur, Gangaikondacholapuram, and Darasuram are UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Site in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) today announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist site during excavations in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The site includes remnants of stupas, viharas, and intricate sculptures, providing new insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India.
• Early findings suggest the site dates back to the Gupta and post-Gupta periods, indicating a thriving Buddhist monastic complex in the region.
• This discovery contributes valuable evidence to understanding the rich cultural and religious heritage of Odisha and its connections with ancient Buddhist centers.
• Further excavations are expected to reveal more about the architectural styles, daily life, and religious practices of the period.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Viharas are Buddhist monasteries.
Odisha has several prominent Buddhist sites like Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri (Diamond Triangle).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding the spread and evolution of Buddhism in India, with special reference to Eastern India.
Analyze how such findings contribute to rewriting or refining existing historical narratives about ancient Indian kingdoms and their cultural interactions.
Examine the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and documenting ancient sites amidst rapid urbanization and development.
Evaluate the role of archaeology in promoting cultural tourism and heritage awareness in India.
GS1.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist stupa with intricate carvings, under the supervision of archaeologists.
GS1.trivia: The famous Buddhist sites of Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri in Odisha collectively form the 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha's Buddhist heritage.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' in Landmark Digital Privacy Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a significant judgment, affirming the 'Right to be Forgotten' in specific contexts of digital privacy and personal data protection.
• The ruling mandates search engines and online platforms to delist or de-index certain personal information upon request, particularly when it is outdated, irrelevant, or causes undue harm.
• The Court balanced the individual's right to privacy against freedom of speech and expression, emphasizing the need for a case-by-case assessment.
• This decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for data protection laws and the obligations of online intermediaries in India.
• It reinforces the principles laid down in the Puttaswamy judgment regarding the right to privacy as a fundamental right.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: Right to be Forgotten is a concept originating from European Union's GDPR.
Puttaswamy judgment declared right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21.
The ruling applies to outdated, irrelevant, or harmful personal information.
Balance struck between privacy and freedom of expression.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Supreme Court's ruling on the 'Right to be Forgotten' in strengthening India's digital privacy framework.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the 'Right to be Forgotten' for online platforms and search engines.
Compare India's approach to data privacy with international standards like GDPR.
Examine the implications of this judgment on freedom of speech and expression in the digital age.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents with a digital screen displaying privacy icons in the background.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' gained prominence after a 2014 European Court of Justice ruling against Google.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership Review Emphasizes Maritime Security and Trade Facilitation
GS2.explainer:
• A high-level review of the India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership concluded today, reaffirming commitment to deeper cooperation across various sectors.
• Key discussions focused on enhancing maritime security cooperation, including joint exercises and information sharing, to ensure a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Both sides stressed the importance of boosting trade and investment, with a renewed push for simplifying trade procedures and exploring new avenues for economic engagement.
• Cultural exchanges, tourism promotion, and cooperation in digital transformation were also identified as priority areas for future collaboration.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy, underscoring its commitment to strengthen ties with the ASEAN region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization of ten Southeast Asian countries.
India became a Sectoral Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a Strategic Partner in 2012.
India's 'Act East Policy' is a successor to the 'Look East Policy'.
Maritime security and trade are key pillars of India-ASEAN cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership in the context of India's 'Act East Policy' and regional geopolitics.
Discuss the potential for enhanced cooperation between India and ASEAN in maritime security and economic integration.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in deepening its engagement with the ASEAN bloc.
Evaluate how India's relationship with ASEAN contributes to its vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states arranged together.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN's motto is 'One Vision, One Identity, One Community'.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Launches New Digital Health Platform for Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) today unveiled a new comprehensive digital health platform specifically designed to enhance healthcare access and delivery in rural and remote areas.
• The platform integrates teleconsultation services, electronic health records (EHRs), and medication delivery networks, aiming to bridge the urban-rural healthcare divide.
• It will facilitate remote diagnostics, specialist consultations, and enable frontline health workers to efficiently manage patient data and outreach programs.
• The initiative is a significant step towards achieving Universal Health Coverage and leveraging technology for equitable healthcare access across India.
• Training programs for healthcare professionals and community health workers on using the new digital tools are also being rolled out.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by subsuming NRHM and NUHM.
The new platform focuses on rural and remote areas.
Key features include teleconsultation and EHRs.
Aims to bridge the urban-rural healthcare gap.
mainsPoints: Discuss how digital health platforms can transform healthcare delivery in rural India, addressing issues of accessibility and quality.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of electronic health records (EHRs) in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate the role of technology in achieving Universal Health Coverage and strengthening primary healthcare systems.
Suggest measures to ensure digital literacy and infrastructure availability for the effective implementation of such initiatives in remote areas.
GS2.imageDescription: A doctor conducting a teleconsultation with a rural patient on a digital tablet.
GS2.trivia: India's eSanjeevani platform has already facilitated millions of teleconsultations, particularly during the COVID-19 pandemic.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Comprehensive UNSC Reforms at Global Forum, Cites Changing Geopolitical Realities
GS2.explainer:
• India today strongly reiterated its call for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) at a multilateral diplomatic forum.
• The Indian representative highlighted that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect contemporary geopolitical realities and the increased role of developing nations.
• India advocated for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent seats, emphasizing the need for greater representation from Africa, Latin America, and Asia.
• The call for reforms aims to make the UNSC more legitimate, effective, and representative of the 21st-century global order.
• India stressed its commitment to multilateralism and the urgent need for a more equitable global governance structure.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
India is a strong contender for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocate for permanent seats.
Veto power is held by the P5 members.
mainsPoints: Analyze the compelling reasons for India's persistent demand for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
Discuss the challenges and obstacles in achieving UNSC reforms, considering the interests of existing permanent members.
Evaluate the potential impact of an expanded and reformed UNSC on global peace and security, particularly for developing nations.
Examine India's role and contributions to global governance that strengthen its case for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations building in New York with flags, symbolizing international diplomacy.
GS2.trivia: The last major reform of the UN Security Council took place in 1965, when the number of non-permanent members was increased from 6 to 10.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Strengthening Data Protection Framework, Calls for Independent Regulator
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) today submitted its report on the proposed Data Protection Bill, recommending several key amendments to strengthen India's data protection framework.
• The committee emphasized the need for a robust and independent data protection authority with significant enforcement powers.
• Recommendations include tighter regulations on cross-border data flows, stricter consent requirements for data processing, and enhanced penalties for data breaches.
• The report also addressed concerns regarding government access to personal data, suggesting clearer guidelines and oversight mechanisms.
• This report is crucial for the finalization of India's long-awaited Personal Data Protection Bill.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Data Protection Bill aims to protect the privacy of individuals' data.
JPC scrutinizes bills and provides recommendations.
An independent Data Protection Authority (DPA) is a key recommendation.
Cross-border data flows and consent mechanisms are critical aspects.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee on the Data Protection Bill and their significance for safeguarding digital privacy in India.
Discuss the role and importance of an independent data protection authority in regulating data processing and ensuring accountability.
Examine the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights in the context of data access by the government.
Evaluate how a robust data protection framework can foster trust in India's digital economy and promote innovation.
GS2.imageDescription: A blurred image of parliamentary proceedings with a focus on a document titled 'Data Protection Report'.
GS2.trivia: The first draft of India's Data Protection Bill was based on the recommendations of the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Nari Shakti Udyam' Scheme to Promote Women Entrepreneurship and Financial Inclusion
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development today launched 'Nari Shakti Udyam', a new scheme aimed at fostering women entrepreneurship and economic empowerment across India.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill development training, and mentorship support to women aspiring to start or expand their businesses.
• It focuses on facilitating access to credit, market linkages, and digital tools for women entrepreneurs, particularly in rural and semi-urban areas.
• 'Nari Shakti Udyam' seeks to break down socio-economic barriers and create a supportive ecosystem for women to achieve financial independence.
• Partnerships with banks, self-help groups (SHGs), and industry associations are integral to the scheme's implementation.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Nodal Ministry is the Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Scheme offers financial aid, skill training, and mentorship.
Aims to promote women's economic independence.
Focus on rural and semi-urban women entrepreneurs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of promoting women entrepreneurship for India's socio-economic development and achieving gender equality.
Analyze how schemes like 'Nari Shakti Udyam' address the unique challenges faced by women entrepreneurs in accessing finance, markets, and skills.
Evaluate the role of self-help groups (SHGs) and digital platforms in empowering women economically.
Suggest further policy interventions and societal changes required to create a truly enabling environment for women entrepreneurs in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of diverse women entrepreneurs confidently discussing business, symbolizing empowerment.
GS2.trivia: India's MUDRA Yojana has been instrumental in providing collateral-free loans to micro-enterprises, with a significant portion going to women beneficiaries.

GS2.headline: Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) Phase 2 Focuses on Circular Economy for Waste Management
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today announced a renewed focus on circular economy principles under Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) Phase 2.
• The initiative aims to transform waste into valuable resources through enhanced segregation at source, advanced processing technologies, and promoting reuse and recycling.
• Key objectives include achieving 100% waste processing, remediation of legacy dumpsites, and making cities 'garbage-free' through a holistic approach.
• Emphasis is placed on citizen participation, behavioral change, and strengthening the capacity of urban local bodies in sustainable waste management.
• The move is expected to significantly reduce environmental pollution, improve public health, and create green jobs in the waste management sector.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) Phase 2 was launched in 2021.
Focus on 'Garbage Free Cities' and wastewater management.
Circular economy principles involve reducing, reusing, and recycling.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the shift towards a circular economy approach in Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) Phase 2 can address India's growing urban waste crisis effectively.
Analyze the challenges in implementing comprehensive waste segregation, processing, and recycling mechanisms across diverse Indian cities.
Evaluate the role of citizen participation, behavioral change, and technological adoption in achieving 'garbage-free' cities.
Suggest policy measures to incentivize private sector involvement and innovation in sustainable waste management practices.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic showing different types of waste being segregated into bins for recycling and composting, with city skyline in background.
GS2.trivia: The Swachh Bharat Mission was launched on October 2, 2014, to achieve universal sanitation coverage.

GS2.headline: States Adopt Unified E-Governance Portal for Seamless Public Services Delivery
GS2.explainer:
• Several Indian states today announced the successful integration and adoption of a unified e-governance portal to provide a wide range of public services to citizens.
• The portal aims to eliminate bureaucratic hurdles, enhance transparency, and ensure efficient, time-bound delivery of government services online.
• Services include applications for various certificates, utility bill payments, land records access, and grievance redressal mechanisms.
• This initiative is part of the broader 'Digital India' vision to transform governance and improve ease of living for citizens.
• Interoperability between different state departments and central government services is a key feature, promoting a 'whole-of-government' approach.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: E-governance aims to make government services accessible, efficient, and transparent.
Digital India is a flagship program to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
The unified portal facilitates citizen-centric service delivery.
Interoperability is key for seamless service provision.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of unified e-governance portals in enhancing public services delivery, transparency, and accountability in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by states in implementing and integrating diverse government services onto a single digital platform.
Evaluate how e-governance initiatives contribute to improving the 'ease of living' for citizens and promoting good governance.
Suggest measures to bridge the digital divide and ensure equitable access to e-governance services across all sections of society.
GS2.imageDescription: A laptop screen displaying a government e-service portal, with hands typing on the keyboard.
GS2.trivia: The National e-Governance Plan (NeGP) was launched in 2006 with the vision of 'Make all Government services accessible to the common man'.

GS3.headline: India Launches Phase II of National Logistics Corridor Project, Boosting Economic Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today inaugurated Phase II of the ambitious National Logistics Corridor Project, focusing on enhancing multimodal connectivity across key industrial and agricultural hubs.
• This phase targets critical infrastructure development, including dedicated freight corridors, port connectivity enhancements, and integrated logistics parks.
• The project aims to significantly reduce logistics costs, improve supply chain efficiency, and boost India's competitiveness in global trade.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging technology for real-time tracking, data analytics, and seamless coordination among various transport modes.
• The initiative is expected to generate substantial employment opportunities and catalyze regional economic development.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Corridor Project aims to reduce logistics costs to global benchmarks.
Phase II focuses on specific geographical corridors and modal integration.
Key components include roads, railways, ports, and digital platforms.
Ministry of Commerce and Industry is the nodal ministry for logistics policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic benefits of improved logistics infrastructure for India's GDP growth and export competitiveness.
Analyze the role of multimodal transport in achieving sustainable and efficient supply chains.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale infrastructure projects like the National Logistics Corridor and suggest mitigation strategies.
Evaluate the project's potential impact on regional disparities and employment generation.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized map showing new logistics corridors connecting major industrial cities and ports in India.
GS3.trivia: India's logistics cost as a percentage of GDP is significantly higher than global averages, making such projects crucial for economic efficiency.

GS3.headline: RBI's Financial Stability Report Highlights Resilience Amidst Global Headwinds, Calls for Vigilance
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today released its Financial Stability Report (FSR) for Q3 2024, assessing the health of India's financial system.
• The report noted continued resilience in the banking sector, characterized by improving asset quality and robust capital adequacy ratios.
• It cautioned against lingering global headwinds, including geopolitical tensions, commodity price volatility, and potential spillovers from advanced economies' monetary policies.
• The FSR emphasized the need for continued vigilance by financial institutions and regulators to address emerging risks, particularly in the non-banking financial company (NBFC) sector and digital lending space.
• Macroeconomic stability and structural reforms were identified as key factors contributing to India's financial robustness.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Financial Stability Report (FSR) is published bi-annually by RBI.
It assesses risks to the financial system.
Key indicators include Gross Non-Performing Assets (GNPA) and Capital to Risk-weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR).
Global headwinds mentioned include geopolitical tensions and commodity prices.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key takeaways from the latest RBI Financial Stability Report regarding the health of India's banking and financial sectors.
Discuss the challenges posed by global economic uncertainties and how India is positioned to mitigate these risks.
Evaluate the role of regulatory vigilance, particularly in the NBFC and digital lending segments, for maintaining financial stability.
Suggest policy measures to further strengthen India's financial system against future shocks.
GS3.imageDescription: A graph showing economic indicators with an RBI logo in the background.
GS3.trivia: The first Financial Stability Report was published by the RBI in March 2010.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious Green Hydrogen Mission Targets for 2030
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy today announced enhanced targets under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for significant production capacity by 2030.
• The revised targets include a substantial increase in green hydrogen production, reduction in GHG emissions, and creation of export opportunities for green hydrogen and its derivatives.
• The mission emphasizes incentives for manufacturing electrolyzers, developing green hydrogen production capabilities, and establishing green hydrogen hubs.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to achieve net-zero emissions by 2070 and establish itself as a global leader in green energy.
• Investments in research and development, skill development, and pilot projects for green hydrogen applications are also integral to the mission.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Infrastructure: Energy.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Targets include reducing fossil fuel imports and creating export opportunities.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of green hydrogen as a key driver for India's energy transition and achieving its net-zero targets.
Discuss the economic and environmental benefits of scaling up green hydrogen production in India.
Examine the challenges in developing a robust green hydrogen ecosystem, including infrastructure, technology, and cost, and suggest policy interventions.
Evaluate India's position as a potential global leader in green hydrogen production and export.
GS3.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing a plant producing green hydrogen with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but green hydrogen production is energy-intensive.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype, Eyes Cost-Effective Space Access
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a critical test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a precise landing demonstration, validating key technologies for autonomous navigation, guidance, and control necessary for future orbital re-entry and landing missions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing fully reusable launch systems, which are crucial for reducing space launch costs and enhancing India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• ISRO aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) RLV capable of ferrying satellites and potentially humans to space.
• The indigenous development of RLV technology underscores India's growing capabilities in advanced space engineering.
